scope: Proposal-only baseline harmonization wording for the AI-agent-ready implementation amendment. No active baseline file is edited by this package.

proposals.title: Clarify that application and AI-agent surfaces do not create model truth
proposals.proposedInsertionLocation: After the source-of-truth / current-state materialization invariants, or in an implementation-facing note section
proposals.proposedText: Application-builder public surfaces, SDKs, capability manifests, tool manifests, traces, previews, read envelopes, and AI-agent outputs are runtime or implementation affordances. They do not create canonical truth, do not promote assertions, do not replace evidence policy, and do not allow projections, caches, compiled outputs, or AI output to become hidden governance decisions. Canonical consequence remains assertion/history-first and governed by accepted commit, promotion, authority, evidence, and pack law.
proposals.rationale: Phase 0-8 created app/agent-facing surfaces. The Constitution should make clear that those surfaces wrap existing truth law rather than altering it.
risks: If worded too broadly, this could appear to introduce new model law rather than clarify implementation boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Does not let SDKs or AI outputs become source-of-truth surfaces.

proposals.title: Add platform public surface and agent-readiness closure to runtime law
proposals.proposedInsertionLocation: After runtime surface / partner-facing service-description material, or as a new AI-agent-ready platform surface subsection
proposals.proposedText: An OFARM Platform implementation may expose public application-builder and AI-agent surfaces only through governed runtime contracts. A public surface set must distinguish public contracts from platform internals; advertise actual capabilities; support preflight, dry-run, explain, and trace retrieval for high-consequence operations where applicable; return registered RuntimeProblem reason codes; preserve result qualification on read and preview surfaces; and generate SDKs only from public contracts. Client applications and AI agents must not mutate canonical stores, materialization stores, authority decisions, pack activation state, or publication records except through governed runtime operations.
proposals.rationale: The platform architecture already requires runtime gates. Phase 0-8 makes those gates agent-callable and testable without changing model truth.
risks: If accepted before runtime evidence, readers may infer that all surfaces are production-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Text remains framed as implementation requirement, not proof of implementation.
Readiness claims point to Phase 8/9 gates.

proposals.title: Record external contract-description patterns as implementation alignment only
proposals.proposedInsertionLocation: New non-substantive alignment row for implementation-facing contract publication
proposals.proposedText: Implementation-facing public contract publication may use OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, JSON Schema, capability/operation catalogues, problem-detail envelopes, and conformance-test descriptors as carrier and tooling patterns. These patterns do not import external semantic law into OFARM, do not override packs or authority policy, and do not allow public schemas or tool annotations to mutate core OFARM meaning.
proposals.rationale: The research supports contract-first tooling, but Phase 9 must prevent external API standards from becoming hidden OFARM semantics.
risks: External standards could be mistaken for normative semantic authority.
acceptanceCriteria: Alignment row explicitly says carrier/tooling only.
No external standard becomes hidden OFARM law.

proposals.title: Update readiness posture without claiming external standard readiness
proposals.proposedInsertionLocation: Readiness posture / remaining bounded debt section
proposals.proposedText: AI-agent-ready implementation support has been drafted as an additive implementation/conformance package: navigation guardrails, public surface drafts, preflight/explain contracts, reason-code registry, result qualification, workflow recipes, practical farm contracts, SDK/API skeletons, and conformance/break tests. This improves implementation direction but does not by itself establish runtime conformance, two-agent compatibility, live deployment evidence, or external standard readiness. Those claims remain blocked until the Phase 8/9 evidence gates are executed and passed.
proposals.rationale: The baseline maturity statement should recognize the draft amendment while preserving the current bounded-debt posture.
risks: If not explicit, the amendment package may be mistaken for proof of runtime readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: Does not claim externally standard-ready status.

proposals.title: Add agent-readiness hostile-review gate
proposals.proposedText: Before any runtime or package is described as AI coding-agent ready, a hostile review must verify that: two independent AI coding agents can build compatible clients from the public contract pack without copying platform internals; semantic-law blocker tests pass; public SDKs expose no internal surfaces; high-consequence operations support preflight/explain/trace where advertised; candidate imports remain candidate-only; result qualification survives through UI/app flows; and no protocol success can waive OFARM semantic-law blockers.
proposals.rationale: The hardest remaining risk is not API syntax; it is AI-agent shortcut behavior that preserves protocol shape while violating OFARM law.
risks: Without this gate, a platform could pass protocol checks but still collapse OFARM semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-agent compatibility remains a hard readiness test.
Semantic-law blockers cannot be waived by API conformance.
